ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sponsorship_levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sponsorship level */,
  "level_name" TEXT /* Name of the sponsorship level (e.g., Platinum, Gold, Silver, Bronze) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sponsorship level and its benefits */,
  "price" TEXT /* The cost associated with each sponsorship level */,
  "max_benefits" INTEGER /* The maximum number of benefits that can be allocated to a sponsor at this level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "benefits" (
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benefit */,
  "benefit_description" TEXT /* Description of the benefit provided to sponsors */,
  "benefit_type" TEXT /* The type of benefit (e.g., branding, registration, booth) */,
  "description_long" TEXT /* A longer description of the benefit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("benefit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sponsorship_benefits" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sponsorship level */,
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the benefit associated with the level */,
  "quantity" TEXT /* Number or description of how the benefit is allocated (e.g., '6 comp', '2') */,
  "allocation_date" TEXT /* The date when the benefit was allocated to the sponsor */,
  "status" TEXT /* The status of the benefit allocation (e.g., pending, allocated, used) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id", "benefit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "registrations" (
  "registration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each registration record */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client who registered */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sponsorship level chosen */,
  "contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the contact person for the sponsor */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address of the contact person */,
  "num_people" INTEGER /* Number of people registered under this sponsorship */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* The date when the sponsor registered */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* The status of the payment (e.g., paid, pending, refunded) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("registration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_registrations_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_registrations_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES sponsorship_levels ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client/sponsor */,
  "client_name" TEXT /* Name of the sponsoring company */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry to which the client belongs */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the client contact person */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the client */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the client */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website URL of the client */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sponsor_communications" (
  "communication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each communication record */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client involved in the communication */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the communication */,
  "date_sent" TEXT /* Date the message was sent */,
  "response" TEXT /* Response from the sponsor to the communication */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date when the sponsor responded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("communication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sponsor_communications_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytic record */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sponsorship level for which analytics is tracked */,
  "registration_count" INTEGER /* Number of registrations associated with the sponsorship level */,
  "feedback_score" REAL /* Feedback score from participants related to this sponsorship */,
  "engagement_metrics" TEXT /* Additional metrics to measure engagement (e.g., social media mentions, booth visits) */,
  "revenue_generated" TEXT /* The revenue generated from the sponsorships */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES sponsorship_levels ("level_id")
)
